# Diegetic Artifact — template
#
# Matches `.claude/skills/diegetic-artifact-generation/SKILL.md` §Output.
# Every diegetic artifact generated by this skill MUST be written as a file
# of this shape. Committed path: worlds/<world-slug>/diegetic-artifacts/<slug>.md
#
# The file is a hybrid: YAML frontmatter (the metadata record — proposal
# Phase 8 schema extended for worldloom) + markdown body (the artifact text
# itself as in-world prose, followed by a demarcated Canon Safety Check
# Trace section).
#
# Required-structural frontmatter fields (do NOT omit):
#   artifact_id, slug, title, artifact_type, author, date, place, audience,
#   communicative_purpose, desired_relation_to_truth, author_profile,
#   epistemic_horizon, claim_map, world_consistency, source_basis
#
# A file missing any of those fails Phase 8 Test 11 (schema completeness).

---
artifact_id: DA-0000                         # canonical artifact ID, monotonic, DA-NNNN
slug: ""                                     # kebab-case of title, lowercase, punctuation-stripped
title: ""                                    # in-world title of the artifact
artifact_type: ""                            # chronicle | sermon | travelogue | herbal | cult_tract | legal_decree | funerary_inscription | manual | letter | folk_tale | fragmentary_myth | prison_confession | scholarly_dispute | battle_song | practical_treatise | grimoire_fragment | relic_manual | other_derived_from_world
statement_of_existence: ""                   # one-line description of what physically exists in-world: paper, stone tablet, wax seal, oral performance record, woven pattern, scratched cave wall, etc.

author: ""                                   # attributed author's name in-world (may be anonymous, pseudonymous, pen name, or institutional — "the charter-house of Port Serekh")
author_character_id: null                    # CHAR-NNNN if character_path was used to lift author; else null
date: ""                                     # world-calendar date per SEC-TML records (e.g., "Year 412 After Flood")
place: ""                                    # ENT/SEC-GEO-bound place of production (settlement, region, or institutional site)
audience: ""                                 # SEC-INS stratum / SEC-ELF demographic / SEC-PAS cluster (not "the public")
communicative_purpose: ""                    # legitimize | warn | memorialize | instruct | accuse | propitiate | narrate | contest
desired_relation_to_truth: ""                # accurate | biased_but_mostly_true | propaganda | mythicized | fragmentary | deliberately_false_in_places
world_relation:                              # explicit relation to canon facts; omit if relation is purely implicit in claim_map
  corroborates: []                           # CF-NNNN list of canon facts this artifact reinforces
  contests: []                               # CF-NNNN list this artifact disputes
  conceals: []                               # CF-NNNN list this artifact hides or obscures
  mythologizes: []                           # CF-NNNN list this artifact transforms into legend
  ritualizes: []                             # CF-NNNN list this artifact embeds in ritual/ceremony

# SOFT inputs — null is acceptable if unspecified; Phase 0 records derivation source in `notes` when defaulted
canon_facts_accessible: null                 # explicit CF list if user-specified; null means "derived from author epistemic horizon" (note in `notes`)
taboo_censorship_conditions: null            # explicit conditions if user-specified; null means "derived from author ideology + INSTITUTIONS taboo" (note in `notes`)
desired_length: null
emotional_tone: null
rhetorical_style: null
ornament_level: null
mystery_seeding_intent: null                 # if true, artifact deliberately gestures at (but does not resolve) a Mystery Reserve entry
contradiction_target: null                   # DA-id this artifact is meant to contradict, if any

genre_conventions:                           # Phase 2 output — conventions this artifact honors or deliberately breaks
  honors: []
  breaks: []                                 # each break must have a Phase 5 author-motive justification in `notes`

author_profile:                              # Phase 0b output — the 15 author fields (trauma_history_if_relevant and sex/gender may be null when not narratively relevant)
  species: ""                                # SEC-PAS cluster binding
  age_band: ""
  sex_or_gender: null                        # nullable when not relevant
  class: ""                                  # SEC-INS stratum
  literacy: ""                               # level + scripts (e.g., "trade-tongue + ritual script")
  profession: ""                             # SEC-INS / SEC-ECR binding
  religious_ideological_environment: ""
  political_dependency: ""                   # patronage, charter, guild, crown, clandestine
  bodily_limits: ""
  mobility: ""                               # SEC-GEO range
  archive_access: ""                         # what institutional records / libraries / scriptoria they can read
  rumor_access: ""                           # what informal channels they hear from
  speech_register: ""                        # formal | vernacular | terse | florid | devotional | legal | colloquial
  likely_blind_spots: ""                     # what their class / profession / ideology makes invisible
  trauma_history_if_relevant: null           # nullable when not narratively relevant

epistemic_horizon:                           # Phase 1 output — author's knowledge surface
  direct_knowledge: []                       # things the author witnessed / experienced / handled
  inferred_knowledge: []                     # things the author can plausibly deduce from training
  secondhand_knowledge: []                   # things the author heard from named or typed sources
  wrongly_believed: []                       # things the author believes but are false / contested — NEVER may match MR disallowed cheap answers (Phase 7b firewall)
  concealable: []                            # things the author knows but would conceal given political_dependency / audience
  impossible_knowledge: []                   # things outside this author's reach (spatial / institutional / epistemic)

claim_map:                                   # Phase 3 output — every claim considered for the artifact, including rejected ones
  - claim: ""                                # the assertion in the author's voice (not paraphrased neutrally)
    canon_status: ""                         # canonically_true | canonically_false | partially_true | contested | mystery_adjacent | prohibited_for_this_artifact
    narrator_belief: ""                      # true | false | uncertain | performed_belief
    source: ""                               # witnessed | learned_from_authority | inherited_tradition | common_rumor | contested_scholarship | impossible_for_narrator_to_verify
    contradiction_risk: ""                   # none | soft | hard
    mode: ""                                 # direct | implied | symbolic
    adaptive_behavior_preserved_under_wrong_ontology: false # optional; true when wrong explanation preserves correct adaptive behavior (Phase 8 Test 12)
    cf_id: null                              # required if canon_status == canonically_true; must resolve via mcp__worldloom__get_record
    mr_id: null                              # required if canon_status == mystery_adjacent; must resolve via mcp__worldloom__get_record
    repair_trace: null                       # if this claim was repaired at Phase 7f, record the repair type + reason here

canon_links: []                              # flat list of CF-ids the artifact touches (for ledger-side indexing)
cannot_know: []                              # explicit list of MR-ids + CAU-3-style restricted items this narrator cannot know — paired with epistemic_horizon.impossible_knowledge but indexed

world_consistency:                           # audit trail — what was checked, what was found
  canon_facts_consulted: []                  # every CF-id read at Phase 3 or Phase 7c
  invariants_respected: []                   # every invariant id tested at Phase 7a
  mystery_reserve_firewall: []               # every MR-id checked at Phase 7b, regardless of overlap (proof-of-check)
  distribution_exceptions: []                # list of "<CF-id>: <Phase 0b embedding that justifies the exception>"

source_basis:
  world_slug: ""
  brief_path: ""                             # path to the user-supplied brief
  character_path: null                       # path to the dossier if character_path was used; else null
  generated_date: ""                         # ISO date, e.g., 2026-04-18
  user_approved: false                       # set true immediately before the Phase 9 artifact-file write

notes: >
  Free-form notes: SOFT-input derivation sources, Phase 7f repair sub-passes that fired,
  deliberate genre_conventions breaks with author-motive justification, trauma_history
  relevance reasoning, sex_or_gender null reasoning, any cross-artifact relationships
  (contradiction targets, sequel-to, citation-of).
---

# <Artifact Title>

<!--
ARTIFACT BODY goes here. This is the text as it would exist in-world,
in the author's voice, with Phase 4 texture embedded and Phase 5
distortions baked in. No editorial framing. No scare quotes around
claims the narrator believes. No parenthetical "(this is of course
false)" notes.

Formatting depends on the artifact_type:
  - chronicle / treatise / sermon / letter → continuous prose
  - funerary inscription / decree → inscription-register prose or list form
  - herbal / manual → entry-by-entry structured list
  - folk tale / myth → narrative prose
  - battle song / verse → verse form
  - fragmentary artifact → partial text with deliberate [lacunae] or [...]
    marking material loss; the loss itself is diegetic, not an LLM
    shortcut

The artifact IS the text. Audit trails live below in the Canon Safety
Check Trace section, and in the frontmatter — never in the body.
-->

---

## Canon Safety Check Trace

<!--
Maintainer-facing audit section. NOT part of the in-world artifact.
Populated at Phase 7 + Phase 8. Clearly demarcated above by a horizontal
rule so a future reader can see where the diegetic text ends and the
audit begins.
-->

### Phase 7a: Invariant Conformance

- **<INVARIANT-ID>**: PASS — <one-line rationale citing the specific claim or capability tested and the invariant's relevant clause>
- ...

### Phase 7b: Mystery Reserve Firewall

- **<MR-ID>**: <PASS | REPAIRED> — <overlap-or-no-overlap status; if overlap, which common-interpretation the artifact references and which disallowed-cheap-answer was avoided>
- ...

No `direct_knowledge` / `inferred_knowledge` / `wrongly_believed` / `claim_map` item matches any MR entry's `disallowed cheap answers` list. Verified.

### Phase 7c: Distribution/Scope Conformance

- **<Author capability>**: <CF-id> — PASS (fits who_can_do_it) | REPAIRED (Phase 0b embedding: <...>) | N/A (ordinary-person scope).
- **<World-fact claim>**: <CF-id> — PASS (author access plausible per archive_access / rumor_access; claim mode consistent with CF distribution).
- ...

### Phase 7d: Diegetic Safety Sub-Check

- **7d.1 No silent canon creation**: PASS — <list of any new named entities introduced and their author-personal-scope bound>
- **7d.2 No restricted-knowledge leakage**: PASS — <restricted vocabulary that appears + Phase 0b institutional access that permits it>
- **7d.3 No local-as-global by accident**: PASS — <any overgeneralizations present + their narrator-plausibility justification + claim_map tagging>
- **7d.4 No untagged intentional contradiction**: PASS — <any deliberate contradictions + their claim_map tagging as canonically_false + narrator_belief>

### Phase 7e: Truth Discipline Sub-Check

- **World-Truth Check**: PASS — <every mode:direct + canonically_true claim cites a valid CF-id>
- **Narrator-Truth Check**: PASS — <register / vocabulary / rhetorical moves consistent with Phase 0b profile>

### Phase 8 Validation Checklist

- Test 1 (Rule 2 / Phase 0 / input binding): PASS — <every HARD input binds to a specific named world entity; SOFT defaults noted in `notes`>
- Test 2 (Rule 2 / Phase 4 / texture citation): PASS — <texture elements cite source files and world-embedded reasons>
- Test 3 (Phase 0b / author profile completeness): PASS — <all 15 author-profile fields populated; any nulls deliberate with noted reasoning>
- Test 4 (Phase 1 / claim source tags): PASS — <every candidate claim has one of the six source tags>
- Test 5 (Rule 7 / firewall non-empty): PASS — <mystery_reserve_firewall lists every MR checked, overlap or not>
- Test 6 (Rule 7 / no forbidden-answer leakage): PASS — <no body / claim_map / wrongly_believed content matches MR disallowed cheap answers>
- Test 7 (Phase 7a / invariants listed): PASS — <invariants_respected lists every invariant tested>
- Test 8 (Rule 4 / Phase 7c / distribution conformance): PASS — <every author capability + world-fact claim respects CF distribution>
- Test 9 (Phase 7d / four diegetic-safety rules): PASS — <each of the four rules named and verified>
- Test 10 (Phase 7e / truth discipline): PASS — <World-Truth + Narrator-Truth both verified>
- Test 11 (Schema completeness): PASS — <no frontmatter field left TODO/placeholder/empty; body non-empty; trace populated>
- Test 12 (Contested Canon / adaptive-but-wrong): PASS — <tagged adaptive-but-wrong claim cited with wrong ontology + canon-true behavior, OR NONE/N/A rationale supplied>

### Phase 7f Repair Sub-Passes Fired

<!-- If any repairs fired, record each as:
- <claim or field>: <repair type> — preserved: <brief intent> / sacrificed: <what was retagged, rescoped, moved, or removed> — <justification>
If none fired, state: "No Phase 7f repair sub-passes fired. All Phase 7 sub-checks passed clean on first pass."
-->
